stem: A 68-year-old post-menopausal woman is referred after a DXA scan. She has no history of fragility fractures. Her only medication is vitamin D 1000 IU daily and dietary calcium intake is adequate. She has no history of oesophageal disease, renal impairment, or malabsorption.

data_table.Investigations: DXA: T-score -2.8 lumbar spine, -2.1 femoral neck. Calcium 2.35 mmol/L (2.15-2.55). 25-OH vitamin D 68 nmol/L. eGFR 72 mL/min/1.73m2. TSH 2.1 mU/L (0.4-4.0).

lead_in: What is the most appropriate initial pharmacological therapy?

options.text: Denosumab 60 mg subcutaneously every six months
options.rationale: Denosumab is effective but is reserved for patients who are intolerant of or have contraindications to oral bisphosphonates, or who have failed bisphosphonate therapy. It is not first-line in this uncomplicated presentation. (Source: RACGP Osteoporosis Guidelines)

options.text: Raloxifene 60 mg orally once daily with calcium
options.rationale: Raloxifene reduces vertebral fracture risk but has no proven benefit for hip fractures. It also carries VTE risk. Bisphosphonates have broader fracture reduction evidence and are preferred first-line. (Source: Osteoporosis Australia; eTG)

options.text: Alendronate 70 mg orally once weekly with calcium
options.rationale: Alendronate is the first-line pharmacological treatment for post-menopausal osteoporosis with T-score of -2.5 or below. It reduces vertebral, hip, and non-vertebral fractures. She has no contraindications to oral bisphosphonates. (Source: RACGP Osteoporosis Guidelines; eTG)

options.text: Zoledronic acid 5 mg intravenously once yearly
options.rationale: IV zoledronic acid is an alternative bisphosphonate typically reserved for patients who cannot tolerate oral formulations, have poor adherence, or have upper GI contraindications. Oral bisphosphonate is trialled first. (Source: RACGP Osteoporosis Guidelines)

Teriparatide is an anabolic agent reserved for severe osteoporosis with multiple fractures or failure of anti-resorptive therapy. PBS-subsidised criteria are strict and this patient does not meet them. (Source: PBS Schedule; AMH)

explanation.summary: Post-menopausal osteoporosis is defined by a T-score of -2.5 or below at the lumbar spine or femoral neck on DXA, or by a minimal trauma fracture regardless of T-score. First-line pharmacological treatment is an oral bisphosphonate, with alendronate being the most commonly prescribed. Adequate calcium and vitamin D intake are ensured concurrently. Denosumab, IV bisphosphonates, and anabolic agents are reserved for specific indications including bisphosphonate intolerance or severe disease.
key_points: T-score of -2.5 or below at spine or hip establishes osteoporosis diagnosis
Oral bisphosphonates are first-line; denosumab is second-line
Alendronate must be taken upright with water 30 minutes before food
PBS criteria for teriparatide require severe osteoporosis with fractures
explanation.pearls: Alendronate reduces hip fracture risk by approximately 40% over 3-4 years in women with established osteoporosis, with efficacy maintained for at least 10 years of continuous therapy per the FLEX extension trial.
